engineIntelligence.h2: Mazda AZ-Wagon Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Mazda AZ-Wagon in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data. All prices reflect grey import kei car market ranges.

cards.altText: Mazda AZ-Wagon K6A engine - petrol 0.658L replacement UK
knownFailures: Ignition coil failure - causes misfire on one or more cylinders, common after 60k miles.
ISCV carbon buildup - idle speed control valve clogs, causing unstable idle at traffic lights.
Injector clogging - fuel injectors degrade, leading to rough running and power loss.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £800-£1,200 (supply only) Estimated from comparable K6A units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Mazda AZ-Wagon K6AT engine - petrol 0.658L replacement UK
knownFailures: Turbo wastegate failure - boost control issues common on high-mileage turbo units.
Turbo bearing wear - blue smoke on acceleration indicates bearing failure requiring rebuild.
Ignition coil failure - turbo variants experience higher coil stress, failing around 50k miles.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £950-£1,400 (supply only) Estimated from comparable K6AT units - actual quotes may vary.

cards.altText: Mazda AZ-Wagon F6A engine - petrol 0.657L replacement UK
knownFailures: Distributor ignition failure - early F6A units suffer distributor cap and rotor wear.
Head gasket seepage - coolant loss possible on high-mileage F6A engines over 80k miles.
Timing belt wear - non-interference but belt replacement recommended at 60k miles.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £750-£1,100 (supply only) Estimated from comparable F6A units - actual quotes may vary.

commonProblems.h2: Mazda AZ-Wagon Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mazda AZ-Wagon's K6A and F6A kei engines are generally robust but suffer from ignition coil failure, ISCV carbon buildup, and turbo issues on boosted variants. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your imported kei car.

problems.h4: Ignition Coil Failure - Why Mazda AZ-Wagon K6A Engines Misfire
problems.affectedModels: Mazda AZ-Wagon Standard, Custom, Turbo (2003-2012, K6A & K6AT)
problems.rootCause: Heat and vibration degrade coil insulation over time, causing intermittent spark loss on one or more cylinders.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty ignition coil pack; diagnostic check included
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix; other coils may fail soon after

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all three ignition coils with upgraded units
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents repeat failures; recommended for high-mileage K6A

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of rebuilt K6A with new coils, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has other wear; eliminates coil worries

problems.recommendation: For a Mazda AZ-Wagon worth £800-£1,500, replacing just the faulty coil makes sense if the engine is otherwise healthy. If the car has over 70k miles or shows other signs of wear, a rebuilt K6A at £800-£1,200 often represents better long-term value.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K6A engine prices for your Mazda AZ-Wagon

problems.h4: ISCV Carbon Buildup - Why Mazda AZ-Wagon Engines Idle Roughly
problems.affectedModels: Mazda AZ-Wagon all variants (2003-2012, K6A & F6A)
problems.rootCause: Carbon deposits accumulate in the Idle Speed Control Valve, restricting airflow and causing unstable idle or stalling.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and ultrasonically clean ISCV valve; reset ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if carbon is the only issue; may recur

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new or reconditioned ISCV valve with gaskets
repairOptions.longevity: Longer-lasting solution; recommended for recurring issues

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of rebuilt K6A with new ISCV, throttle body cleaned, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if engine has multiple issues or high mileage

problems.recommendation: ISCV cleaning is a cost-effective first step for rough idle on a Mazda AZ-Wagon. If symptoms persist after cleaning, replacement of the ISCV is usually the right call before considering a full engine replacement.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mazda AZ-Wagon K6A engine with new ISCV

problems.h4: Turbo Failure - Why Mazda AZ-Wagon K6AT Engines Lose Boost
problems.affectedModels: Mazda AZ-Wagon Turbo, Custom Turbo (2004-2012, K6AT)
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger bearings wear from heat and oil starvation; wastegate actuators stick, causing over/under-boost.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace wastegate actuator or linkage; boost test
repairOptions.longevity: Quick fix for boost control issues only

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Rebuild turbocharger with new bearings, seals, balance
repairOptions.longevity: Restores turbo performance; suitable if engine block is sound

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of rebuilt K6AT with new/rebuilt turbo, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine shows other wear or high mileage

problems.recommendation: Turbo rebuilds on the K6AT are viable if the engine block is healthy and oil maintenance has been good. For a Mazda AZ-Wagon Turbo over 60k miles, a rebuilt K6AT engine at £950-£1,400 often makes more sense than repairing an aged turbo on a worn engine.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt K6AT turbo engine prices for your Mazda AZ-Wagon

problems.h4: Injector Failure - Why Mazda AZ-Wagon Engines Lose Power
problems.rootCause: Fuel injectors clog from carbon or wear internally, causing lean/rich running, misfire, and power loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Ultrasonic clean all injectors; flow test; replace seals
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for mild clogging; may not fix worn injectors

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fit new or reconditioned injector set; ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Reliable long-term fix; recommended for high-mileage engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of rebuilt K6A with new injectors, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best if engine has multiple issues or compression loss

problems.recommendation: Injector cleaning is worth trying first on a Mazda AZ-Wagon with mild power loss. If cleaning doesn't resolve symptoms or the engine has over 70k miles, replacing the injector set or opting for a rebuilt engine offers better reliability.
problems.cta: Find Mazda AZ-Wagon engine with new injectors

problems.group: Is My Mazda AZ-Wagon Worth Repairing?
problems.h4: Is My Mazda AZ-Wagon Worth Repairing? - The Value Decision
problems.rootCause: Low residual value of imported kei cars makes major repairs economically challenging.

repairOptions.longevity: Makes sense if car is rust-free and otherwise sound
Only worthwhile if car has low miles and good bodywork

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply of rebuilt engine with 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if you want to keep the car long-term

problems.recommendation: For a Mazda AZ-Wagon worth under £1,000, minor repairs are usually the sensible choice. If the car is rust-free, low-mileage, and you plan to keep it, a rebuilt engine at £750-£1,400 can extend its life significantly and represent better value than buying another grey import.
problems.cta: Get a tailored quote for your Mazda AZ-Wagon engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mazda AZ-Wagon
finalCta.paragraph: The Mazda AZ-Wagon's kei engines are compact and efficient, but ignition, turbo, and injector issues can leave your import stranded. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who understand grey import kei cars. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get your AZ-Wagon back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mazda AZ-Wagon Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mazda AZ-Wagon and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Mazda AZ-Wagon Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mazda AZ-Wagon's production timeline, organised so UK grey import owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: Early AZ-Wagon models used the F6A engine family, with turbo options available on select trims.
keyChanges: Introduction of Mazda-badged Wagon R as AZ-Wagon for Japanese domestic market
F6A SOHC engine standard; F6AT turbo optional on higher trims
Transition from MD11S to MD22S chassis in 2000 with minor updates
knownFor: Simple SOHC design makes F6A easy to service for import specialists
Distributor ignition wear common on high-mileage early units
Strong compatibility with Suzuki Wagon R parts expands UK availability

years.description: The K6A DOHC engine replaced the F6A, bringing improved efficiency and becoming the most common AZ-Wagon engine in UK imports.
keyChanges: K6A DOHC 12-valve engine introduced across the range
Turbo variant (K6AT) offered on Custom trim with 64hp output
MJ22S late-model update added 4WD option with same K6A base
knownFor: Ignition coil failure emerges as dominant replacement driver after 60k miles
ISCV carbon buildup causes unstable idle on high-mileage K6A units
K6A shares design with Suzuki Wagon R, simplifying UK parts sourcing

faq.h2: Mazda AZ-Wagon Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Mazda AZ-Wagon engine in the UK?
items.answer: Mazda AZ-Wagon K6A engine replacement typically costs £800-£1,200 for a rebuilt unit (supply only), while used engines range £600-£900. Prices reflect the grey import kei car market and vary by engine code, mileage and supplier.
keyPoints: Rebuilt K6A engines cost £800-£1,200 supply only from UK specialists
Used K6A engines available £600-£900 depending on mileage and condition
Turbo K6AT variants cost £950-£1,400 rebuilt due to turbocharger complexity
Supply & fit service typically adds £300-£600 to engine price
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mazda AZ-Wagon engine prices today

items.question: Which engine code does my Mazda AZ-Wagon have?
items.answer: Most UK-imported Mazda AZ-Wagons use the K6A (0.658L) or F6A (0.657L) engine code. Check your chassis plate or registration lookup to confirm the exact code for your variant.
keyPoints: K6A code fits third/fourth gen AZ-Wagons (MJ21S/MJ22S/MJ23S, 2003-2012)
F6A code fits first/second gen models (MD11S/MD12S/MD22S, 1998-2003)
Turbo variants use K6AT or F6AT suffix on the base engine code
Enter your registration above for instant engine code identification
items.cta: Get your Mazda AZ-Wagon engine code identified instantly

items.question: Are Mazda AZ-Wagon engines interchangeable with Suzuki Wagon R?
items.answer: Yes - the Mazda AZ-Wagon uses identical Suzuki-sourced K6A and F6A engines, making Wagon R engines direct replacements for AZ-Wagon models with matching chassis codes.
keyPoints: K6A and F6A engines are shared between Mazda AZ-Wagon and Suzuki Wagon R
Chassis code matching (MJ21S/MJ22S/MJ23S) ensures correct fitment
Suzuki Wagon R engines expand UK availability for AZ-Wagon replacements
Always verify transmission and 4WD compatibility before ordering
items.cta: Find Suzuki Wagon R K6A engines compatible with your AZ-Wagon

items.question: What causes ignition coil failure on Mazda AZ-Wagon K6A engines?
items.answer: Heat and vibration degrade ignition coil insulation over time, causing intermittent spark loss. Failure commonly occurs between 50,000-80,000 miles on K6A engines.
keyPoints: Ignition coils fail due to thermal cycling and engine bay vibration
Symptoms include misfire, rough idle, and check engine light
Replacing all three coils prevents repeat failures on high-mileage engines
Upgraded coil packs available through UK specialists reduce recurrence
items.cta: Compare Mazda AZ-Wagon K6A engines with upgraded ignition coils

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Mazda AZ-Wagon?
items.answer: For rust-free, low-mileage AZ-Wagons valued over £1,000, a rebuilt engine at £800-£1,200 can extend the car's life significantly. For lower-value examples, used engines or repair may offer better value.
keyPoints: Typical AZ-Wagon values range £800-£1,500 for running examples
Rebuilt K6A engines cost £800-£1,200 with 12-month warranty
Used engines £600-£900 suit budget repairs or short-term ownership
Consider body condition and rust before committing to engine replacement
items.cta: Get a tailored quote for your Mazda AZ-Wagon engine decision

items.question: Can I fit a Mazda AZ-Wagon engine myself?
items.answer: While physically possible, K6A/F6A engine replacement requires specialist knowledge of kei car mounting, wiring and ECU adaptation. We recommend professional fitting by import-specialist workshops.
keyPoints: K6A engines are compact but require precise mounting and wiring harness connection
ECU adaptation and immobiliser matching need specialist diagnostic tools
UK kei car specialists offer supply & fit services from £300-£600 added cost
Professional fitting ensures warranty validity and correct post-installation testing
items.cta: Find Mazda AZ-Wagon engine supply & fit specialists near you

items.question: What warranty comes with Mazda AZ-Wagon replacement engines?
items.answer: All rebuilt and reconditioned Mazda AZ-Wagon engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists. Used engine warranties vary by supplier.
keyPoints: Rebuilt/reconditioned engines: minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Used engines: typically 3-6 month warranty depending on supplier
Warranty covers manufacturing defects and premature component failure
Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: Compare Mazda AZ-Wagon engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: How long does Mazda AZ-Wagon engine replacement take?
items.answer: Delivery of a rebuilt K6A engine typically takes 3-7 working days from UK stock. Professional fitting requires 1-2 days depending on workshop scheduling and any ancillary part requirements.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines in UK stock: 3-7 day delivery to your chosen workshop
Used engines from Japanese import: 10-21 day shipping plus customs clearance
Professional fitting: 1-2 working days once engine is delivered
Allow extra time for ECU adaptation and road testing post-installation
items.cta: Get quotes with delivery timelines for your Mazda AZ-Wagon engine

items.question: Will a K6A turbo engine fit my non-turbo Mazda AZ-Wagon?
items.answer: Physically yes - K6A and K6AT share the same block and mounting points. However, turbo conversion requires additional wiring, intercooler piping, ECU remapping and exhaust modifications for reliable operation.
keyPoints: K6A and K6AT engines share identical block dimensions and mounting
Turbo conversion needs wiring harness, intercooler, exhaust and ECU changes
Cost of conversion often exceeds buying a factory turbo AZ-Wagon
Stick with matching NA or turbo engine for straightforward replacement
items.cta: Compare like-for-like Mazda AZ-Wagon engine replacements

items.question: What's the most reliable engine for a Mazda AZ-Wagon?
items.answer: The naturally aspirated K6A (54hp) is generally more reliable than the turbo K6AT, with fewer failure points and lower replacement costs. Early F6A engines are robust but less powerful and harder to source in the UK.
keyPoints: K6A NA engines have fewer failure points than turbo K6AT variants
Ignition coil and ISCV issues affect all K6A engines but are easily addressed
F6A engines are robust but distributor wear and parts availability are concerns
Choose rebuilt K6A NA for best balance of reliability, cost and UK availability
items.cta: Find reliable rebuilt K6A engines for your Mazda AZ-Wagon
